title: senecio erucifolius var. arenarius
intro: Senecio erucifolius var. arenarius, also known as the sea ragwort, is a variant of the ragwort plant, characterized by its succulent leaves and ability to thrive in coastal environments. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Senecio erucifolius var. arenarius include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs and congestion, and supporting immune function, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
